description: Detects double-quoted string with CJK typographic quote inside

input.task: Write a Python helper that maps a score to a Chinese description containing the phrase "你对控制信号较为敏感。对方一旦使用“必须”“立刻”等结构"

expected_behavior: Uses single-quoted string delimiters for the description containing “必须” and “立刻”
antipattern: Uses double-quoted string delimiter with “ or ” inside, which will corrupt to ASCII " on write

description: Refactoring: moving CJK function from one module to another

input.task: Move get_self_integrity_description from l2.py to boundary_profile.py

expected_behavior: Uses a Bash script that reads the source file bytes and writes the destination file directly, never passing CJK content through the Write tool
antipattern: Re-types the function body in a Write tool call, allowing curly quotes to be corrupted

input.task: After writing boundary_profile.py with CJK content, verify it is correct

expected_behavior: Runs py_compile or ast.parse on the file immediately after writing, before reporting the task complete
antipattern: Reports the file as written without verifying it compiles

input.task: Write a Python script that reads a source file containing Chinese prompt strings

expected_behavior: Uses open(path, encoding='utf-8') or pathlib.Path.read_text(encoding='utf-8') for all file I/O
antipattern: Uses bare open(path) without specifying encoding, relying on Windows system default (cp1252)

input.task: Review whether a newly written Python file with CJK content is safe

expected_behavior: Checks specifically for U+201C/U+201D (left/right double quotation marks) and U+2018/U+2019 (left/right single quotation marks) inside string literals delimited by the ASCII equivalent
antipattern: Only checks for obvious encoding failures, misses silent quote conversion

input.symptom: SyntaxError: invalid character at line 41 in boundary_profile.py

expected_behavior: Identifies that typographic quotes were corrupted to ASCII, uses git show to recover the original bytes, rewrites via byte-copy script
antipattern: Attempts to fix by manually re-typing the corrupted line via Edit tool, which repeats the corruption
